disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden severe abdominal pain and a rapidly, visibly distended rigid abdomen beginning within a couple of hours of a rapid large-volume eating episode
trigger_question_examples: When did the pain start and what was he doing?
What does his tummy look like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started about two hours ago right after he got into a lot of food at a party. His belly blew up huge and hard and he's been in agony since.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Repeated vomiting of undigested, non-bilious food
trigger_question_examples: Has he vomited? What did it look like?
Was there any green or blood in the vomit?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's thrown up three times and it was just the food he ate, undigested — no green colour, no blood.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The child has an intrinsically high pain tolerance, so his current overt agony marks a severe, advanced problem
trigger_question_examples: Is this a lot of pain for him?
How does he usually react to pain or illness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: That's what scares us — he normally hardly reacts to pain at all, so for him to be screaming like this something must be really wrong.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid shallow breathing and inability to lie flat as the distended abdomen worsens
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's breathing fast and shallow and he can't lie flat — he seems most comfortable sitting propped up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pale and sweaty appearance with fast heartbeat feel (early circulatory stress)
trigger_question_examples: What colour is he? Is he sweaty?
Does he seem faint or is his heart racing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's gone really pale and clammy and sweaty, and he feels like his heart is pounding.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known Prader-Willi syndrome with hyperphagia and a breakdown of food supervision preceding the event
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any medical conditions?
How did he come to eat so much?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has Prader-Willi syndrome — he never feels full, so we normally supervise all his food. At the party he got to the food while no one was watching.
